critical_newsletters: The Age of Async Agents — Cognition's Walden Yan & OpenInspect's Cole Murray

key_themes: Agent Architecture & Orchestration: background/async agents, Dynamic Workflows, brain-sandbox separation, subagent patterns

Agent Memory & Context: GBrain persistent memory, LangChain Delta Channels (40x compression), context management
Agent Benchmarks & Evaluation: DeepSWE, ITBench-AA, AgingBench, howtoeval.com, ProgramBench multi-agent quality/speed
Agent Training: Trajectory (continuous post-training), Polar (agent-harness fine-tuning), Cursor RL training
Model Improvements for Agents: Opus 4.8 honesty calibration, Qwen 3.7 Max 35-hour agent sessions
AI Industry Structure: Altman/Amodei softening job displacement predictions, enterprise sales as % of headcount

classifications.subject: The Age of Async Agents — Cognition's Walden Yan & OpenInspect's Cole Murray
wiki_relevance: Three waves of AI coding: IDE assistants → local agents → async/background agents — foundational taxonomy
Devin architecture deep-dive: brain/sandbox separation, Firecracker VMs, BlockDev filesystem, nested virtualization
OpenInspect: open-source background agent system (GitHub app, Slack bot, MCP, code review bot)
Harness in-the-box vs out-of-the-box architectural tradeoffs — security, state management, secrets handling
Devin internal metrics: 16%→80% commits in 3 months, 7x PR growth, 10% headcount increase
Model inflection point (Dec 2025): Opus 4.5 & GPT-5.2 enabled spec→PR with minimal handholding
Repo setup as hardest problem: Docker, Docker-in-Docker, mock services, local dev environments
Testing ≠ Computer Use: multi-model orchestration for end-to-end testing
GitHub integration: Devin reads/replies to PR comments, can disagree with reviewers
Coding agent economics: $20/seat vs full-stack ownership debate

classifications.subject: [AINews] Anthropic raises $965B Series H, releases Opus 4.8 and Dynamic Workflows/ultracode
wiki_relevance: Anthropic Series H: $65B raised at $965B valuation, $47B revenue run-rate — historic AI funding event
Claude Opus 4.8: SWE-Bench Pro 69.2% (+10pts vs GPT-5.5), 15% fewer turns, 35% fewer output tokens
Dynamic Workflows / ultracode: Claude Code multi-agent orchestration — hundreds of parallel subagents
Bun case study: 750K LOC Zig→Rust rewrite, 99.8% tests passing, 11 days, hundreds of parallel agents
Opus 4.8 honesty calibration: 4x less likely to let code flaws pass, lowest hallucination rate
Multi-agent system findings: reaching mediocre solutions 2x faster, not necessarily better final quality
ProgramBench: multi-agent quality vs speed tradeoff quantification
